title: US CPI Forecast Snapshot — June 2026 (v3.1 EIA-Validated Methodology)
methodology: Two-stage aggregation using exact BLS 2024-2025 relative importance weights. Stage 1: component-level forecasts. Stage 2: weighted aggregation. Verification: Food+Energy+Core=100.000% and Shelter+CoreGoods+CoreServices=80.557%. Energy components validated with AAA National Average (EIA API 403). WTI futures NOT used for gasoline CPI per backtest (R²=0.23, MAE=8.1%). Backtest: 15 months weight accuracy MAE 0.035% core, 0.146% headline.
summary: June CPI headline flat 0.0% m/m (consensus +0.1%) on oil collapse; core +0.2% m/m (consensus +0.25%). WTI crashed from $107 to $75 in June. AAA gas $3.86 (-12% from May avg). Fed dot plot 3.8% = zero cuts in 2026. FOMC hawkish limits soft-print reaction. Energy deflation is the dominant headline driver.
baseCase: Flat headline CPI on oil collapse; core stable at +0.2% but FOMC hawkishness caps market reaction

aggregation.verification: Food + Energy + Core = 100.000% AND Shelter + CoreGoods + CoreServices = 80.557%

release.name: CPI
release.thesis: WTI crashed from $107 to $75 in June (-28%). AAA gas $3.86 vs May avg $4.39 (-12%). Energy CPI deflation = -3.0% m/m. Exact BLS weights: Food 13.25% + Energy 6.19% + Core 80.56%. Core stable (+0.2%) with shelter cooling but services sticky. FOMC dot plot 3.8% = zero cuts in 2026. Hawkish Warsh limits soft-print market reaction.

scenarios.label: Base case
scenarios.outcome: Headline 0.0% m/m, Core +0.2% m/m — below consensus on oil deflation
scenarios.marketReadthrough: 10Y yields fade slightly (4.40-4.45%), DXY 100-101 stabil. Airlines (UAL) and chemicals (LYB) rally on jet fuel/naphtha margin expansion. Energy (XLE) and gold (GLD) under pressure. Tech (NVDA) limited upside due to FOMC hawkishness.
scenarios.invalidation: Iran deal collapses, WTI spikes above $85, or core services re-accelerate above +0.30%.

scenarios.label: Hotter inflation surprise
scenarios.outcome: Headline +0.2% m/m, Core +0.3% m/m — oil rebounds, services sticky
scenarios.marketReadthrough: 10Y yields spike (4.55%+), DXY 101.5+. Tech (NVDA) and REITs (AMT) selloff. USD long, energy (XLE) short-cover rally. Gold (GLD) squeeze.
scenarios.invalidation: Hot print driven by one-off energy component; core services actually stable.

scenarios.label: Cooler disinflation impulse
scenarios.outcome: Headline -0.1% m/m, Core +0.15% m/m — oil collapse fully captured, DXY compression
scenarios.marketReadthrough: 10Y yields drop (4.30-4.35%), DXY 99.5-100. Growth (QQQ) rallies, airlines (UAL) +10-15%. Homebuilders (DHI) on mortgage relief. Energy (XLE) -5%.
scenarios.invalidation: Market already priced a benign outcome; FOMC hawkish tone prevents rally.

setups.bias: Long duration on cooler or inline core
setups.trigger: Core CPI <= +0.2% and services ex-shelter stable; WTI stays below $80
setups.invalidation: Sticky core services above +0.30% with wages re-accelerating.

setups.bias: Cautious — FOMC hawkish limits multiple expansion
setups.trigger: 10Y yield drops below 4.40% after release and DXY softens below 100
setups.invalidation: Hot CPI with 10Y yield above 4.55% and DXY above 101.5.

setups.bias: Long — jet fuel collapse + summer demand
setups.trigger: WTI $72-78 and CPI headline soft; summer travel bookings strong
setups.invalidation: Iran deal collapses, WTI above $85, or travel demand disappoints.

setups.bias: Short — oil supply glut, Iran deal progress, demand weak
setups.trigger: WTI below $80 and Hormuz traffic normalizes; API inventory weak demand
setups.invalidation: Iran deal collapses, Israel attacks, or OPEC+ cuts production.

setups.bias: Short — DXY 100.85 + FOMC hawkish + real yields rising
setups.trigger: DXY above 100.50 and 10Y yield above 4.45% on CPI day
setups.invalidation: DXY drops below 99.5 or geopolitical shock (Iran deal collapse).

watchItems.label: Fed path / Warsh
watchItems.note: FOMC hawkish hold on 16-17 Jun. Warsh first meeting. 9/19 policymakers expect hike. PCE forecasts revised up to 3.6%.

watchItems.label: 10Y UST
watchItems.note: Key regime line. Below 4.40% = growth rally. Above 4.55% = risk-off. Oil pulling down, FOMC pushing up.

watchItems.label: WTI Oil / Energy Data
watchItems.note: WTI crashed -28% in June. AAA gas $3.86 (Jun 29) vs $4.39 (May). EIA API 403 — AAA used as primary. BACKTEST WARNING: WTI futures NOT used for gasoline CPI (R²=0.23). Energy weight only 6.193% but gasoline swing is large.

watchItems.label: Iran Deal / Hormuz
watchItems.note: US-Iran deal signing weighed on crude. Hormuz reopening negotiations ongoing. Supply glut risk = oil downside.

watchItems.label: DXY
watchItems.note: Strong dollar = import deflation on core goods. Partially offsets PPI passthrough.

watchItems.label: Data Quality / Backtest
watchItems.note: Backtest (15 months): exact BLS weights Core MAE 0.035%, Headline MAE 0.146%. WTI→Gasoline model failed (R²=0.23, MAE=8.1%). AAA National Average used as EIA API unavailable.

keyDrivers: Oil crash: WTI -28% in June ($107→$75) = gasoline CPI -8% to -10% m/m. AAA national average $3.86 vs May $4.39. Energy deflation is the dominant headline driver. EIA API 403 — AAA validated.
FOMC hawkish hold: Warsh's first meeting. Dot plot 3.8% median = zero cuts in 2026. PCE forecasts revised up to 3.6%. Limits soft-CPI market reaction.
Iran deal progress: US-Iran agreement signed, Hormuz reopening talks. Supply glut risk = oil downside. WTI broke $80 support.
DXY 100.85: Strong dollar = import deflation on core goods. Partially offsets PPI passthrough. Core goods may see mild deflation.
Core services sticky: Retail sales resilient, wages +3.6% YoY. But shelter decelerating (Zillow/Apartment List). OER normalizing to ~0.25% m/m.
risks: Iran deal collapse: Nuclear inspectors denied, Israel/Lebanon tensions, US Congress opposition. WTI $85+ = CPI revision to hotter.
Core services re-acceleration: If wages or transportation services spike above +0.35%, core undershoot narrative breaks.
FOMC hawkish limits reaction: Market may 'sell the rally' even on soft CPI. Warsh 'one data point' rhetoric. Zero cuts in 2026 priced.
Demand weakness: Global recession fears rising. If oil and risk assets both fall, soft CPI = bad news for equities.
Shelter lag uncertainty: Zillow/Apartment List slowing but BLS 6-12 month lag. Faster deceleration = core downside surprise.
